Alteration) Le\'el: 7 Component s: V, S, M Range: Special Casting Time: 7 Duration: I round per level Saving T hrow: None Area of Effect: Special Definition: When this spell is cast, the wiza rd causes an oval , glowing ball of silvery hue to form in the air over (I to 2 feet above) his or her head. It floats there si lently unlil the spell expires, and need not be further concentrated upon to maintain ils existence. Any spell, magi­ cal power , or magical item effect that is cast directly upon the w izard (as opposed to area­ effect magic) will be attracted to, and utterly absorbed by, this floating spell/rap . This includes magic missiles 
[PDF_PAGE 10 | score=12] Definition: This spell creates a wall of swirling, opaque sand I-foot thick per level ofthc caster, 12 feet high, and 4 fcctlon g pcr lcvel ofthc caster. It must be cast so that it rests on a solid surface and must remain in contact with that surface, although the caster may move it about. The sand is thick and choking; insects cannot fly through iI, but are bat­ tered to death, their wings crus hed. All creatures save thosc with infravision will bc rendercd blind for as long as they are within Ihe wall's confines, and are - 2 on attack rolls and - 3 on AC on the round after they emerge from the wall, as they try 10 draw breath, clear their eyes, ears, mouths. and noses of sand, and so on. Fashioned from the bones of creatu res who died violently, they appea r only as hilts ca rved of bone. [PDF_PAGE 13 | score=11] which involves a piece of snakeskin (or a ponion ofa dead snake) which is consumed by the magic, the caster instantly teleports snakes to his or her location. These living snakes appear in the caster's mouth, and are vomited out. They do not choke or othe rwise harm the cas ter, and never attack a caster either. Until the spell expires, they serve the caster, fighting to the death. The snakes are nor­ mal, not giant var ieties, and may be both ven­ omous and constric tors. One sna ke appe ars for every three levels of the caster (rounding down); a maximum of six serpents can issue from a caster's mouth in anyone round. Until all of them have been expelled out of the mouth, speech, spellcast­

Each power of the watch is followed with a parenthetical number that indicate s the number of leve ls of life energy that the watc h must drain . Thus, if it we re used to invoke a featherfall (I) spell and then a haste (3) spell by an elf, the owner would have to seek out and kill four Ist- (or 0-) level elves, two 2nd-level elves, or some o ther combination that would total four levels. The use of any of the watch's spells or spe ll-like abilities precludes taking another action in the same round . The magical spe lls that are availab le to the holder of the t
